steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $f(x)=30(2)^{x}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Since both terms share $(2)^{x}$: $$f(x)=22(2)^{x}+8(2)^{x}=30(2)^{x}.$$ The coefficient $30$ now equals the y-intercept.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
We need a form that displays $30$ as a coefficient or base.
  • A) ${88}(2)^{x-2}+32(2)^{x-2}$ — equivalent, but $30$ does not appear; $f(0)=88/4+32/4=22+8=30$ is hidden.
  • B) ${2}(11(2)^{x}+4(2)^{x})$ — coefficients $2,11,4$; $30$ is not displayed.
  • C) ${22}(4)^{x/2}+8(4)^{x/2}$ — uses base $4$; $30$ is not displayed.
  • D) ${30}(2)^{x}$ — the coefficient $30$ is exactly the y-intercept.
That confirms D is correct.
